title: nerve-wml: Substrate-Agnostic Nerve Protocol for Inter-WML Communication
description: nerve-wml defines a substrate-agnostic communication protocol between World Model Language (WML) units. Two concrete substrates (MlpWML dense MLP, LifWML surrogate-gradient leaky-integrate-and-fire SNN) share the same Nerve Protocol, enabling measured cross-substrate polymorphism (0% gap on linearly separable FlowProxyTask, 12.1% gap on HardFlowProxyTask, Gate M merge retaining 1.000 of mock-baseline accuracy). The repository provides the reference substrate-portability measurement cited in the parent research program's Paper 1 v0.2 section 7.4 (dreamOfkiki cross-substrate validation).
